decks.notes: The focus of this UWx Control deck is to heavily disrupt and control the opponent's game plan through counterspells, removal spells, and card draw. Utilizing cards like Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, the deck aims to protect its own threats while disrupting the opponent's plays. Playing efficiently with cards like Brainstorm and Preordain, as well as gaining card advantage with cards like Murktide Regent and Flow State, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and control the game until it can establish a dominant position.

The main focus of this deck is to establish a fast-paced aggressive strategy with a mix of creature and spell-based threats. The deck aims to quickly apply pressure on the opponent through efficient creatures like Amped Raptor and Guide of Souls while disrupting their game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Cabal Therapy. Its mana base is designed to support its aggressive game plan while providing flexibility with access to different colors of mana. Overall, the deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of early aggression and disruption.

The deck revolves around utilizing lands as control elements, using cards like Thespian's Stage, Dark Depths, and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's game plan. It also incorporates cards like Crop Rotation and Life from the Loam to fetch key lands or recycle resources. The inclusion of Boseiju, Who Endures and Mox Diamond provide additional protection and ramp, while cards like Ghost Quarter and Maze of Ith offer defensive options. Overall, the deck aims to lock down the game by leveraging powerful land interactions and resource management.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and efficiently get powerful creatures into play from the graveyard using various spells like Reanimate, Animate Dead, and Shallow Grave. Cards like Unmask, Thoughtseize, and Cabal Therapy help to disrupt the opponent's hand and graveyard. Griselbrand is a key creature to reanimate, providing card draw and life gain. The deck also includes other powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Atraxa, Grand Unifier to overwhelm opponents. The goal is to use the combo pieces to win the game before the opponent can mount a defense.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to create a control-based prison strategy, locking down opponents' abilities to play spells or activate abilities. It aims to ramp up quickly with cards like Ancient Tomb and Grim Monolith, and then secure the lock with cards like Karn, the Great Creator, Ugin, the Eye of the Storms, and Trinisphere. The deck also includes strong artifact threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger to close out the game. Overall, the key concept is to establish artifact-based control and lock opponents out of the game.

The main concept of this deck revolves around using artifacts to control the game and create a prison-like effect for the opponent. By utilizing cards like Candelabra of Tawnos, Trinisphere, and Planar Nexus, the deck aims to restrict the opponent's options and slow down their game plan. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Karn, the Great Creator, and Ugin, Eye of the Storms provide further control and disruption. With powerful artifacts like The One Ring and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn as win conditions, this deck is designed to outlast and outmaneuver the opponent in a Legacy 1 vs 1 format.

The deck focuses on aggressive plays and disrupting the opponent's game plan through counterspells and land destruction. It utilizes cheap card draw and filtering to maintain tempo and pressure on the opponent. The creatures in the deck are efficient threats that can quickly close out games. The deck also includes versatile spells that can deal with various threats or provide additional card advantage. Overall, the deck aims to control the game early on and finish off the opponent swiftly with its aggressive creatures and burn spells.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive army of low-cost creatures known as "Weenies". The deck utilizes cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Guide of Souls to boost the power of these creatures, while also using cards like Swords to Plowshares and Wasteland to control the opponent's board. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Karakas to disrupt the opponent's plays. Overall, the deck aims to quickly build up a strong board presence and overwhelm the opponent before they can react effectively.

The main concept of this UWx control deck is to maintain control of the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive abilities. By utilizing cards like Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Stifle, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while deploying threats like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Murktide Regent to close out the game. The deck also has card advantage engines like Ponder and Quantum Riddler to ensure a steady stream of resources. Additionally, the mana denial strategy with cards like Wasteland and Daze further adds to the deck's control elements.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays and efficient removal spells to overwhelm the opponent quickly. Using cards like Ajani, Guide of Souls, and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, the deck aims to establish a fast board presence and press the opponent with powerful attacks. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Swords to Plowshares and Static Prison provides disruption and control elements to handle opposing threats. The deck is designed to apply constant pressure and maintain tempo throughout the game to secure a swift victory.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly drop powerful Eldrazi creatures on the battlefield and overwhelm opponents with aggressive plays. It utilizes powerful mana acceleration through cards like Eye of Ugin and Lotus Petal to bring out threats like Thought-Knot Seer and It That Heralds the End early in the game. Chalice of the Void and Eldrazi Temple help to further ramp up the deck's explosive potential, while cards like Glaring Fleshraker and Eldrazi Linebreaker provide additional pressure to finish off opponents swiftly. The goal is to establish board dominance and win before the opponent can mount a defense.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game through land destruction with cards like Wasteland and Gaea's Cradle, while ramping up mana with cards like Birds of Paradise and Green Sun's Zenith to eventually overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures like Craterhoof Behemoth. The deck also utilizes cards like Endurance and Collector Ouphe to disrupt the opponent's strategy and protect its own game plan. Overall, the strategy focuses on resource management and building a strong board presence to secure victory.

This deck focuses on using aggressive creatures to disrupt the opponent's game plan while also utilizing disruptive cards like Swords to Plowshares and Thoughtseize. The synergy between cards like Stoneforge Mystic, Aether Vial, and Recruiter of the Guard allows for consistent and powerful plays. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion provides additional value and flexibility. With a mix of removal, disruption, and efficient creatures, this deck aims to pressure the opponent while controlling the game state.

The deck focuses on locking down opponents with a combination of artifact prison cards like Planar Nexus, Trinisphere, and Karn, the Great Creator. It utilizes powerful artifacts like Candelabra of Tawnos and Urza's Workshop to generate mana and control the board. The inclusion of cards like Ugin, Eye of the Storms and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn provide additional win conditions. The deck aims to control the game through a combination of artifact synergies, disruption, and powerful threats, ultimately overwhelming the opponent and leading to victory.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and aggressively get Arclight Phoenix into play while disrupting the opponent's strategies with cards like Force of Will and Thoughtseize. The deck utilizes cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to accelerate the casting of spells, while also having card selection and card draw with Ponder, Brainstorm, and Preordain. Additionally, the deck has removal spells like Lightning Bolt and strong threats like Murktide Regent to maintain pressure on the opponent. The mana base is centered around fetching for the necessary dual lands to support the deck's spells.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to generate mana and control the board while quickly building a strong board presence to overwhelm the opponent. It includes cards like Mox Opal and Mishra's Bauble to ramp up mana production, Emry, Lurker of the Loch to recur artifacts from the graveyard, and Urza's Saga to create powerful Artifact tokens. Chalice of the Void and Force of Will serve as disruption and protection, allowing the deck to maintain pressure and tempo. Overall, the strategy revolves around leveraging artifact synergies for a quick and aggressive win.
